verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is canonically the beta subunit of the F1 catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase, residing in the mitochondrial matrix. However, there is well-documented evidence that it translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells and on endothelial cells (as ecto-ATP synthase), where it participates in extracellular ATP synthesis and has been targeted by antibodies on the cell surface. This cancer/cell-state-induced ecto-display qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced surface accessibility, even though the dominant localization is mitochondrial.
